title: lupinus sulphureus var. subsaccatus
intro: Lupinus sulphureus var. subsaccatus, also known as the yellow bush lupine, is a perennial shrub native to the coastal regions of California and Oregon. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which can aid in treating wounds, skin irritations, and digestive issues. The health benefits of Lupinus sulphureus var. subsaccatus include its ability to reduce inflammation, alleviate pain, and promote digestive health. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, and its flowers, leaves, and stems can be used in various herbal remedies, including salves and topical ointments.
